ActivityPanelBlock.description:
``ui.activity:`` block — opt-in observability pane for sub-agents.

Surfaces the live sub-agent fan-out, background tasks, and recent
terminal events as a dedicated workspace mode (any client). Pure display: the daemon never inspects this; both
clients consume the field through the manifest summary and gate
the Activity mode entry on its presence.

**Opt-in contract**: when this block is omitted (default), the
Activity mode is HIDDEN from the workspace mode menu and the
panel is not rendered. A simple chat app that never spawns
sub-agents shouldn't see it. Apps that orchestrate fan-out
(coordinator, dev assistant, multi-agent research) opt in.

Example YAML::

    ui:
      activity:
        enabled: true
        position: right
        title: "Activity"
        show_running: true
        show_recent: true
        show_stats: true
        show_bg_tasks: true
        max_recent: 50
        auto_open_on_spawn: false

auto_open_on_spawn.description: When true, the client auto-switches to the Activity pane the first time the agent spawns a sub-agent. Off by default — surface only when the user opens it explicitly.
auto_open_on_spawn.title: Auto Open On Spawn

enabled.description: Master switch. When this block is present in YAML the client renders the Activity mode entry. Set ``enabled: false`` to disable the pane while keeping the rest of the config (useful for staged rollouts).
enabled.title: Enabled

max_recent.description: Cap on the number of terminal events kept in the recent list. Older events are evicted FIFO. Tune up for long-running orchestration sessions; the panel's performance degrades past ~200.
max_recent.title: Max Recent

position.description: Where the activity pane sits relative to the chat: right | bottom | overlay. Mirror of ``WorkspaceBlock.position`` semantics.
position.title: Position

show_bg_tasks.description: Interleave background shell tasks alongside sub-agents in the live + recent strips. Disable for apps that only spawn sub-agents (no shell.bash run_in_background).
show_bg_tasks.title: Show Bg Tasks

show_recent.description: Render the recent-terminal-events scrollable list. Carries the last ``max_recent`` agents that completed / failed / cancelled, with one-line preview.
show_recent.title: Show Recent

show_running.description: Render the live sub-agent strip at the top of the pane. Set false to suppress and keep only the recent + stats sections (useful for archival / read-only views).
show_running.title: Show Running

show_stats.description: Render the aggregate stats footer (total spawned / completed / failed, average duration, success rate). Pulls from ``/api/metrics`` digitorn_agent_* counters.
show_stats.title: Show Stats

title.description: Panel header label. Defaults to a localised ``Activity`` string when omitted.
title.title: Title

ActivityPanelBlock.title: ActivityPanelBlock

AgentBrain.description:
LLM brain configuration for an agent.

Two modes:

1. **Inline** - full provider config embedded in the agent::

    brain:
      provider: deepseek
      model: deepseek-chat
      temperature: 0.2
      config:
        api_key: "{{secret.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY}}"
        base_url: "https://api.deepseek.com/v1"

2. **Reference** - points to a named provider in ``modules.llm_provider``::

    brain:
      provider_id: deepseek_main
      temperature: 0.2

backend.description: Provider backend: 'anthropic', 'openai_compat', or 'github_copilot' (uses your Copilot subscription via api.githubcopilot.com).
backend.title: Backend

context.description: Context window management for this brain. If not set, inherits from execution.context. Useful in multi-agent apps where each brain uses a different model.

credential.description:
Reference to a user-vault credential. Two YAML shapes:
  - string (compact): `credential: openai_main`
  - mapping (explicit): `credential: { ref: openai_main, scope: per_user }`
The runtime resolves the reference at activation time and injects the credential's fields into `config` (api_key, base_url, etc.) replacing any inline values. Recommended over inline `{{secret.X}}` templates.
credential.title: Credential

fallback.description:
Fallback brain used when the primary provider returns a billing or credit error (402, insufficient balance). Lets apps gracefully degrade to a cheaper/free model instead of failing. Example:
  fallback:
    provider: anthropic
    model: claude-haiku-4-5
    config:
      api_key: "claude-code"

image_detail.description: Image resolution for vision. 'auto' = provider decides. 'low' = 512px (cheaper, faster). 'high' = native resolution (more accurate, more tokens).
image_detail.title: Image Detail

image_generation.description: Whether this model can generate images. If true, the framework handles image output in tool results and SSE events. Models like DALL-E, Stable Diffusion via MCP.
image_generation.title: Image Generation

max_images_per_turn.description: Max images sent to the model per turn (0 = unlimited).
max_images_per_turn.title: Max Images Per Turn

native_tool_use.description: Override native tool calling detection. By default, auto-detected from provider (e.g. Ollama defaults to text-based). Set to true to force native OpenAI-style tool_calls (e.g. qwen2.5-coder on Ollama). Set to false to force text-based tool calling.
native_tool_use.title: Native Tool Use

provider.description: Provider hint for auto-resolving base URL. Known values: anthropic, openai, deepseek, groq, mistral, together, ollama, lm_studio, vllm, google-gemini, gemini, xai, grok, cerebras, perplexity, fireworks, github_copilot.
provider.title: Provider

provider_id.description: Reference to a named provider declared in modules.llm_provider.config.providers. If set, provider/model/config are ignored.
provider_id.title: Provider Id

vision.description: Whether this model supports image input (vision). null = auto-detect from model name. true = force enabled. false = convert images to text descriptions.
vision.title: Vision

AgentBrain.title: AgentBrain

AgentDefinition.description:
Definition of a single agent in the app YAML.

Only ``id`` and ``brain`` are required for now.
Other fields (tools, signals, loop, watch) will be added
when we implement the full agent runtime.

Example::

    agents:
      - id: coordinator
        role: coordinator
        brain:
          provider: deepseek
          model: deepseek-chat
          temperature: 0.2
          config:
            api_key: "{{secret.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY}}"
            base_url: "https://api.deepseek.com/v1"
        system_prompt: |
          You are a coordinator agent.

brain.description: LLM provider configuration for this agent.

capabilities.description: List of skill names to auto-load from the bundle's ``skills/`` directory. The compiler reads ``skills/<name>.md`` for each entry and appends the content to this agent's ``system_prompt`` under an ``## Available capabilities`` section. Clean way to separate the agent's identity (system_prompt) from its skill definitions (individual markdown files).
capabilities.title: Capabilities

coordination.description: Grouped orchestration block: delegate_to + pool. Replaces the historical scatter.

delegate_to.description: Agent IDs this coordinator can delegate to. The compiler verifies each entry references a declared agent id.
delegate_to.title: Delegate To

hooks.description: Per-agent hooks - merged with ``execution.hooks`` but only evaluated when this specific agent is active. Use for specialist-specific behavior (e.g. a `reviewer` agent that runs extra lint, a `writer` agent that logs every edit). App-wide hooks still fire for every agent; these add on top.
hooks.title: Hooks

id.description: Unique agent identifier within this app.
id.title: Id

instructions.description: Grouped prompt-extension block: file + capabilities + specialty. Replaces the historical scatter.

modules.description:
Modules this specialist can access. Empty = same as coordinator.
Supports two formats (mix is OK):
  - Simple: ['filesystem', 'shell'] - full module access
  - Granular: [{filesystem: [read, grep]}, 'shell'] - restrict actions
modules.title: Modules

plan_first.description: When true, the agent must explain its plan in plain text before executing any tools on the first turn. Prevents silent tool calls.
plan_first.title: Plan First

pool.description: Agent pool config for coordinators (max_workers, progress, auto_retry).

role.description: Agent role hint. Functional roles: 'coordinator' (can spawn agents), 'specialist' (pre-configured expert), 'worker' (default). Descriptive roles like 'assistant', 'analyst', 'reviewer' are also accepted and used in the system prompt.
role.title: Role

skills.description: Path to a .md file with detailed methodology/instructions for this specialist.
skills.title: Skills

specialty.description: Short description of this specialist's expertise (shown to coordinator).
specialty.title: Specialty

system_prompt.description: System prompt injected at conversation start.
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

AgentDefinition.title: AgentDefinition

AgentNode.description:
Run an existing declared agent for one turn.

The agent is identified by the ``agent`` field which must reference
a declared ``agents[].id`` (validated in
:func:`validate_flow_references`).

description.description: Free-form description, surfaced on the canvas tooltip.
description.title: Description

id.description: Unique node identifier within the flow.
id.title: Id

input.description: Static or templated input passed to the agent.
input.title: Input

on_error.description: Error-handling edges. Catch-all (default: true) must be last.
on_error.title: On Error

AgentPoolConfig.description:
Pool configuration for coordinator agents that spawn specialists.

Replaces the old ``dict[str, Any]`` form which used to be validated
only by hand in the compiler. The compiler-side check is now a
no-op - Pydantic enforces the shape and bounds.

auto_retry.description: Number of automatic retries when a specialist fails.
auto_retry.title: Auto Retry

max_workers.description: Maximum concurrent specialist agents this coordinator can spawn.
max_workers.title: Max Workers

progress.description: Whether to relay progress events from spawned agents to the coordinator.
progress.title: Progress

AgentPoolConfig.title: AgentPoolConfig

attachments.description:
Attachment types the chat composer accepts.
  - ``image``    PNG / JPG / GIF / WEBP / HEIC, routed to a vision-capable LLM
  - ``document`` PDF / TXT / MD / CSV / XLSX / DOCX / code files, extracted as text before being handed to the model
  - ``audio``    MP3 / WAV / M4A / OGG, transcribed via STT
  - ``video``    MP4 / MOV / WEBM, accepted by a few vision models (Gemini, Sonnet)

Pass the string ``*`` to enable every supported type. Leaving the field empty (``null`` / unset) disables attachments entirely: the composer's ``+`` menu collapses to slash-commands + snippets only. Apps must opt in - the default is no attachments.
attachments.title: Attachments

category.description: App category for grouping in the UI. Examples: 'coding', 'writing', 'research', 'data', 'devops', 'design', 'communication', 'automation', 'general'.
category.title: Category

color.description: Accent color for the app card/header. Hex format: '#8B5CF6'. If empty, auto-generated from app_id hash.
color.title: Color

features.description: Client-UI feature toggles (same contract as top-level features:). Keys: voice, attachments, tools_panel, snippets, tasks_panel, memory_panel, context_ring, markdown, slash_commands, message_actions, status_pills, token_badges. Unspecified keys default to true on the client.
features.title: Features

icon.description: App icon. Can be: emoji ('💻'), icon name ('code'), URL to an image ('https://...'), or base64 data URI. If empty, the client generates a colored circle from app_id.
icon.title: Icon

quick_prompts.description: Suggested prompts shown as clickable buttons when the user opens the app. Each entry: {label: 'short text', message: 'full prompt', icon: 'emoji'}. If empty, the client shows just the input field.
quick_prompts.title: Quick Prompts

schema_version.description: YAML schema version for forward compatibility.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

short_name.description: Compact label used in tight UI slots (dashboard chip, tab header, mobile drawer). One word, or two SHORT words; longer names overflow the 68 px chip and overlap their neighbors. When empty, clients fall back to `name` (truncated with ellipsis).
short_name.title: Short Name

theme.description: Client theme overrides. Keys: accent (hex), background (hex). accent overrides app.color for fine-grained control.
theme.title: Theme

ApprovalNode.description:
Human-in-the-loop gate. Pauses until a human chooses an option.

The decision becomes part of the flow context as
``approvals.<node_id>`` so downstream routes can branch on it via
``when: "approvals.<id> == 'approve'"``.

choices.description: Selectable answers. The user picks one.
choices.title: Choices

description.description: Free-form description, surfaced on the canvas tooltip.
description.title: Description

id.description: Unique node identifier within the flow.
id.title: Id

on_error.description: Error-handling edges. Catch-all (default: true) must be last.
on_error.title: On Error

ApprovalNode.title: ApprovalNode

BehaviorConfig.description:
Behavioral enforcement rules - actively monitored at runtime.

Define a profile preset and/or individual rules. All enabled rules
are enforced by the behavior engine on every tool call.

Example::

    behavior:
      profile: coding
      classify_turns: true
      classifier:
        frequency: every_turn
        timeout: 15
        approaches: [direct, plan_and_confirm, delegate]
      rules:
        read_before_edit: true
        test_after_changes: true
      custom:
        - id: protect_migrations
          rule: "Never modify migration files without asking"
          trigger: edit
          action: block

brain.description:
LLM for semantic classification. Uses the same AgentBrain format as agents.
If omitted, uses the coordinator's brain.
Tip: use a fast/cheap model (haiku, deepseek-chat) for minimal latency.

classifier.description: Configuration for the semantic classifier LLM.

classify_turns.description: Enable semantic classification. A small LLM analyzes each user message BEFORE the main agent acts and injects behavioral directives.
classify_turns.title: Classify Turns

custom.description: Legacy custom rules (backward compat). Prefer rule_definitions.
custom.title: Custom

rule_definitions.description: Fully declarative rules - works for ANY action. See BehaviorRuleDefinition.
rule_definitions.title: Rule Definitions

rules.description: Rule overrides. Keys are rule IDs (read_before_edit, test_after_changes, etc.), values are bool or int.
rules.title: Rules

state_tracking.description: What the session state tracks. When null, uses defaults from profile.

use_agent_brain.description: When brain is not set, reuse the coordinator's brain for classification. Set to false to disable classification when no brain is configured.
use_agent_brain.title: Use Agent Brain

BehaviorConfig.title: BehaviorConfig

BehaviorCustomRule.description:
Legacy custom rule format. Kept for backward compatibility.
Prefer ``rule_definitions`` for new apps.
BehaviorCustomRule.title: BehaviorCustomRule

BehaviorRuleDefinition.description:
A fully declarative behavioral rule - works for ANY action.

Example::

    rule_definitions:
      - id: read_before_edit
        description: "Must read a file before editing it"
        trigger: [edit]
        when: pre_tool
        action: warn
        condition:
          target_not_in_set: read_files
        message: "You are editing '{target}' without reading it first."

      - id: no_sql_injection
        description: "Block raw SQL in user-facing queries"
        trigger: [database.execute]
        when: pre_tool
        action: block
        condition:
          param_matches:
            param: query
            pattern: ".*;\s*(DROP|DELETE|TRUNCATE)"
        message: "Dangerous SQL detected. Use parameterized queries."

condition.description:
When the rule fires. Condition types:
  target_not_in_set: <set_name>    - target param NOT in tracked set
  target_in_set: <set_name>         - target param IS in tracked set
  counter_gte: {name, value}         - counter >= threshold
  param_matches: {param, pattern}    - param matches regex
  param_contains: {param, value}     - param contains string
  flag_is: {name, value}             - flag equals value
  no_text_before_tools: true         - agent didn't explain before tools
  consecutive_gte: <N>               - same tool called N+ times
  all: [conditions...]               - all must match
  any: [conditions...]               - at least one matches
  not: <condition>                   - negation
condition.title: Condition

message.description:
Message template. Placeholders:
  {target}              - file_path or primary target param
  {tool}                - current tool name
  {param:<name>}        - any param value
  {counter:<name>}      - counter value
  {set_count:<name>}    - size of a tracked set
  {turn}                - current turn number
message.title: Message

trigger.description: Tool name(s) that trigger this rule. '*' = all tools.
trigger.title: Trigger

BehaviorRuleDefinition.title: BehaviorRuleDefinition

approval_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for user approval before auto-denying (30–3600).
approval_timeout.title: Approval Timeout

approve.description: Actions requiring explicit user approval before execution.
approve.title: Approve

hidden_actions.description: Specific actions to hide from the agent's tool index. Unlike 'deny', hidden actions are invisible but still executable by setup steps, hooks, and channels. Use this to declutter the agent's toolset without breaking internal automation.
hidden_actions.title: Hidden Actions

hidden_modules.description: Module IDs to hide from the agent's tool index. Hidden modules are still loaded and can be used by setup steps, hooks, and channels - but the agent cannot see or call their tools. Example: ['filesystem'] to prevent the agent from accessing files.
hidden_modules.title: Hidden Modules

CapabilityGrant.description: An explicit grant or deny for module actions.

actions.description: Action names. Empty = all actions on the module.
actions.title: Actions

CapabilityGrant.title: CapabilityGrant

ChannelInstanceConfig.description:
Configuration for a named output channel instance.

Each entry in the ``channels:`` block defines a channel instance
with a user-chosen name, a channel type, and type-specific config.

Optionally, a ``user_resolver`` auto-resolves per-user delivery targets
(email, phone, chat_id) from a data source - no manual ``output_config``
needed.

Example::

    channels:
      slack_alerts:
        type: webhook
        config:
          url: "{{secret.SLACK_WEBHOOK}}"

      sms_user:
        type: sms
        config:
          account_sid: "{{env.TWILIO_SID}}"
          from_number: "+33600000000"
        user_resolver:
          module: database
          action: fetch_results
          params:
            query: "SELECT phone FROM users WHERE session_id = :session_id"
          mapping:
            to_number: phone

config.description: Channel-specific configuration. Supports {{variables}} and {{secret.X}} / {{env.X}} for credentials. See 'digitorn channel schema <type>' for available fields.
config.title: Config

type.description: Channel type ID. Built-in: 'llm_notification', 'webhook', 'log'. Plugins: 'slack', 'gmail', 'telegram', 'kafka', 'sms', etc. (via pip install digitorn-channel-<type>)
type.title: Type

user_resolver.description: Optional user resolver for auto-targeting notifications. When set, the channel automatically looks up the user's delivery address (email, phone, chat_id) from a data source using the session_id. No manual output_config needed.

ChannelInstanceConfig.title: ChannelInstanceConfig

quick_prompts_visible.description: Show suggested ``ui.quick_prompts`` chips above the composer when the conversation is empty.
quick_prompts_visible.title: Quick Prompts Visible

voice.description: Microphone button. Default ``true`` to match the legacy ``features.voice`` default - apps that want voice off should set ``composer.voice: false`` explicitly.
voice.title: Voice

ChatSideWidget.description: Z2 - companion side panel rendered next to the chat.
ChatSideWidget.title: ChatSideWidget

ChatThinkingBlock.description: Per-app visibility / collapse defaults for ``<thinking>`` blocks.

collapsed_default.description: Initial collapsed state of thinking blocks; the user can still toggle them when ``visible`` is true.
collapsed_default.title: Collapsed Default

visible.description: When false, thinking blocks are hidden entirely.

show_silent.description: When true, plumbing tools (memory ops, agent_spawn internals, discovery meta-tools) are rendered. Default false hides them.
show_silent.title: Show Silent

bubble_style.description: Bubble visual treatment: card | flat | minimal.

ClassifierConfig.description:
Configuration for the semantic classifier LLM.

The classifier is a generic pre-turn analysis engine. Each app
configures what it analyzes, when it runs, and what it produces.

Example::

    behavior:
      classify_turns: true
      classifier:
        frequency: every_turn
        timeout: 15
        complexity_levels: [trivial, simple, moderate, complex, critical]
        approaches: [direct, explore_first, plan_and_confirm, delegate, research_first]
        risk_levels: [none, low, medium, high]
        max_directives: 5
        system_prompt: "{{prompt.classifier}}"

approaches.description:
Approach strategies. Each entry is either a plain string
or a dict with {name, label, when, behavior} for full customization:

  approaches:
    - name: direct
      label: 'Execute directly'
      when: 'Task is trivial or simple, clear path'
      behavior: 'Go straight to tool calls, minimal text'
    - name: ask_expert
      label: 'Needs human expertise'
      when: 'Domain knowledge required that the agent lacks'
      behavior: 'Ask the user with AskUser, explain what you need to know'
approaches.title: Approaches

complexity_levels.description:
Ordered list of complexity levels. Each entry is either a plain string
or a dict with {name, when, behavior} for full customization:

  complexity_levels:
    - name: trivial
      when: '1 action, obvious answer'
      behavior: 'Just do it, no planning'
    - name: deep
      when: 'Cross-cutting concern, 10+ files'
      behavior: 'Full plan, user approval, phased execution'
complexity_levels.title: Complexity Levels

directive_footer.default: Follow these directives. They are based on your behavioral rules and the current session state. Violations are detected in real-time.
directive_footer.description: Text appended at the end of every directive message.
directive_footer.title: Directive Footer

directive_prefix.description:
Format string for the directive header. Available placeholders:
{complexity}, {approach}, {risk}, {approach_label}
directive_prefix.title: Directive Prefix

frequency.description:
When to run the classifier:
  'every_turn'    - before every agent turn (classifier can skip via skip_reason)
  'first_turn'    - only on the first turn of a session
  'every_n_turns' - every N turns (set frequency_n)
  'on_new_message'- only when the user sent a new message (skip tool-only turns)
frequency.title: Frequency

high_risk_threshold.description: Risk level (from risk_levels) at or above which high_risk_warning is appended.
high_risk_threshold.title: High Risk Threshold

high_risk_warning.default: Risk level: {risk}. Confirm destructive or irreversible actions with the user before proceeding.
high_risk_warning.description: Warning appended when risk >= high_risk_threshold. Use {risk} placeholder.
high_risk_warning.title: High Risk Warning

max_directives.description: Maximum number of directives the classifier should produce.
max_directives.title: Max Directives

risk_levels.description:
Risk levels. Same format as approaches - string or dict:

  risk_levels:
    - name: safe
      when: 'Read-only, no side effects'
    - name: destructive
      when: 'Deletes data, drops tables, force-pushes'
      behavior: 'MUST confirm with user, explain what will be lost'
risk_levels.title: Risk Levels

skip_followups.description: Auto-skip classification for simple follow-ups: 'yes', 'ok', 'continue', 'go ahead', etc. Saves a classifier LLM call.
skip_followups.title: Skip Followups

system_prompt.description:
Custom system prompt for the classifier LLM.
Supports {{prompt.X}} references to load from ./prompts/.
When null, uses the built-in default behavioral model.
The prompt receives the output schema dynamically - you don't
need to hardcode complexity/approach values in your prompt.
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for the classifier LLM response.
timeout.title: Timeout

ClassifierConfig.title: ClassifierConfig

ClassifierContextConfig.description: What context the classifier receives about the agent's state.

history_depth.description: How many recent messages to include.
history_depth.title: History Depth

recent_history.description: Send recent messages with tool calls and results.
recent_history.title: Recent History

session_state.description: Send session state: files read/edited, searches, violations, turn number.
session_state.title: Session State

tool_inventory.description: Send the agent's tool names + descriptions.
tool_inventory.title: Tool Inventory

workspace_info.description: Send workspace metadata: project type, languages, file count.
workspace_info.title: Workspace Info

ClassifierContextConfig.title: ClassifierContextConfig

ContextConfig.description:
Context management configuration for the agent loop.

Controls how the context window is managed to prevent overflow errors.
When the context fills up, the runtime can automatically compact it
using the configured strategy.

Can be set at two levels:
- ``execution.context`` - default for all agents
- ``agent.brain.context`` - per-brain override (multi-agent apps)

Example::

    brain:
      provider: deepseek
      model: deepseek-chat
      context:
        max_tokens: 131072
        strategy: summarize
        keep_recent: 30
        compression_trigger: 0.70

auto_compact.description: Enable automatic compaction. When true, the runtime injects a context_pressure hook automatically if none is declared.
auto_compact.title: Auto Compact

compression_trigger.description: Token pressure ratio (0.0-1.0) at which automatic compaction triggers.
compression_trigger.title: Compression Trigger

keep_recent.description: Number of recent messages to preserve during compaction.
keep_recent.title: Keep Recent

max_tokens.description: Context window size in tokens. 0 = auto-detect from provider. Override if the provider doesn't report it.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

output_reserved.description: Tokens reserved for output generation. Subtracted from max_tokens for pressure calculation.
output_reserved.title: Output Reserved

summary_brain.description: Optional separate brain for summarization during compaction. Use a fast/cheap model for summaries instead of the main brain. If not set, the agent's main brain is used.

summary_max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens for the summary when using 'summarize' strategy.
summary_max_tokens.title: Summary Max Tokens

One field inside a credential provider (e.g. ``api_key``, ``bot_token``).
